$comment: Single source of truth for the coordinated release. The npm packages all publish at `version` (sync mode — see RELEASING.md). The model identity (which trained artifact + tokenizer the neural-weights-* packages bundle) lives under `weights`, decoupled from the artifact's historical filename. Paths are relative to `weights.dataRoot` (override the root with MAILWOMAN_DATA_ROOT, or the individual files with MAILWOMAN_PUBLISH_MODEL / MAILWOMAN_PUBLISH_TOKENIZER). Bump `version` here in lockstep with the release; copy-weights.mjs, the ship script, and the model card all read this instead of hardcoding the numbers.

weights.lineage: v1.9.2-multilocale-au (v1.9.1-multilocale-3order + the G-NAF Australia 3-order shard @ source-weight 6.0, country_weights += AU:1.0, step 40000, #208/#148) — the AU word-order fix. 150k real G-NAF rows rendered canonical/pc-first/locality-first (the same i%3 rotation overture proved). AU right-place @25km 65→87 on the demo resolver (candidate-20j + #370 rescore + #194 Lever A); CLI shard 37.5→51.4 @25km (+13.9pp); all-panel @25km 90.0→93.1%. Guardrails held on the shipped US/FR coordinate (gate-clean clean-compile re-grade): us.postcode 97.5, us.locality 73.1, us.street 78.2, de.native_locality 90.7, fr.postcode 99.5, fr.house_number 99.3; mask-regression gate (#718) PASS; int8↔fp32 delta 0. Autonomous-promote canary clean (aggregate +1.7pp, 0.6% new-overconfidence). Ship-config UNCHANGED from v1.9.1. Tokenizer 0.6.0-a0 (byte-identical). int8 md5 ff37551c990e541be48b060a8f7c94f8.

softFeed.$comment: Soft-feed artifacts (#718 D1) materialized into each neural-weights-<locale> workspace at publish time by copy-weights.mjs so the library default `loadFromWeights` feeds the channels the anchor+gazetteer-trained model expects (closing the anchor-OFF crater). The PCB1 postcode binary is built from the WOF postcode shard (per locale's country code); the gazetteer lexicon is the codex-generated repo file. Paths under `db` are relative to weights.dataRoot/wof. Like the weights binaries, these are skipped in CI (MAILWOMAN_SKIP_WEIGHTS_COPY) — the publish workflow places them via the HF fetch step.

assets.$comment: Where the model release lives for the demo + the CI weights pull. The release path embeds `version`; publish.yml derives the same path from the model card so CI and local stay in sync.
